title: Mizoram Governor Leads Combined Celebration of Foundation Days of Bihar, Rajasthan, Odisha and Himachal Pradesh
content:
18/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 10th April, 2026 :Under the aegis of the ‘Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat’ initiative, Mizoram Governor, General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd), today led a combined celebration commemorating the Foundation Days of Bihar, Rajasthan, Odisha and Himachal Pradesh at the Durbar Hall of Lok Bhavan, Aizawl.

In his address, General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh expressed delight at being able to celebrate the foundation days of these four unique states together. Highlighting their rich heritage and significant achievements, he stated, “Each state is unique and excels in its own way. They possess immense potential, and tremendous progress has been made in all these states over the years.” The Governor lauded the 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' initiative and remarked, “Celebrating the foundation day of every State and Union Territory helps us understand one another’s traditions, heritage, and culture. It fosters the spirit of unity in diversity. India, as a whole, is a unique country, and our diversity is our greatest strength, which will guide us towards building a Viksit Bharat by 2047.” He also commended all the performers and participants for their outstanding contributions in making the programme a grand success.

The event was presided by Pi Lalhruaitluangi, Addl. PS to the Governor, who also explained the objective of the programme. Video messages from the respective Governors - Lt. Gen. (Retd) Syed Ata Hasnain (Bihar), Shri Haribhau Kisanrao Bagde (Rajasthan), Dr. Hari Babu Kambhampati (Odisha), and Shri Kavinder Gupta (Himachal Pradesh) - were played, followed by short video presentations highlighting the history and development of each state.

Representatives from the four states also shared brief insights into their states’ progress. They included Dr. Saurav Suman from Bihar, Asst. Professor at NIT Mizoram; Dr. Nidhi Yadav from Rajasthan, Asst. Professor at NIT Mizoram; Prof. Pravakar Rath from Odisha, Pro Vice Chancellor of Mizoram University (MZU) and Ms. Sonali from Himachal Pradesh, a student at the College of Veterinary Sciences & Animal Husbandry, Mizoram. Students from Mizoram University, NIT Mizoram, and Govt. Mizoram Law College enthralled the audience with vibrant cultural dances and folk songs, adding a colourful dimension to the occasion. After the formal programme, the Governor interacted warmly with all participants and guests, who spent a pleasant and memorable time together.

Bihar was carved out of the Bengal Presidency and came into existence on March 22, 1912. Rajasthan was formed on March 30, 1949, by the integration of the princely states of Jodhpur, Jaipur, Bikaner, and Jaisalmer, and was initially known as Greater Rajasthan. Odisha, which was earlier part of Bihar, attained full statehood on April 1, 1936. It is recognised as one of the earliest states formed on linguistic lines in India. Himachal Pradesh was constituted as a Part C State under the Indian Union on April 15, 1948, and was granted full statehood on January 25, 1971.

The 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' programme seeks to strengthen bonds among the people of India’s diverse states and Union Territories through cultural exchange, promoting greater understanding and appreciation of each region’s unique traditions, languages, and lifestyles.

Attachment

title: Mizoram Larsap hovin Bihar, Rajasthan, Odisha leh Himachal Pradesh-te dinchamphaphak lawm
content:
128/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 10th April, 2026 :Vawiin khan 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' hmalakna hnuaiah Mizoram Larsap, General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh, PVSM, AVSM, YSM (Retd) hovin Bihar, Rajasthan, Odisha leh Himachal Pradesh-te state dinchamphaphak lawmna Durbar Hall, Lok Bhavan, Aizawlah neih a ni.

Larsap General (Dr) Vijay Kumar Singh chuan state danglam bik leh pawimawh tak tak pali Bihar, Rajasthan, Odisha leh Himachal Pradesh-te dinchamphaphak lawmna buatsaih a ni chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin hemi pual hian duhsakna a hlan a. Heng state-te chanchin langsar leh chhuanawm tak takte tarlangin, "State tinte hian danglam bikna an nei theuh a, thatna riau an neih mai bakah theihna nasa tak an neih avangin hmasawnna nasa takin heng state-ahte hian a thleng a ni," a ti a. Larsap chuan Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat hmalakna fakawm a tih thu sawiin, "State/UT-te dinchamphaphak kan inlawm sak tawn thin hmang hian midangte hnam ziarang, nunphung leh tih dante kan hriat thuk lehzual phah a, hei hian inpumkhatna siamin India chu ram danglam bik a nih zia a lantir a ni," a ti a. Danglamna nasa tak kara kan inpumkhat tlatna chu kum 2047-a ram changkang leh intodelh, "Viksit Bharat" a India siam tura hmanraw pawimawh tak a nih thu a sawi a. Programme hlawhtlin theih nana tha thawhtu zawng zawngte chhuanawm a tih thu a sawi bawk.

He hun hi Pi Lalhruaitluangi, Addl. PS to Governor chuan kaihruaiin programme buatsaih chhan a sawifiah a. Bihar Larsap Lt. Gen. (Retd) Syed Ata Hasnain, Rajasthan Larsap Shri Haribhau Kisanrao Bagde, Odisha Larsap Dr. Hari Babu Kambhampati leh Himachal Pradesh Larsap Shri Kavinder Gupta te thuchah bakah state palite chanchin tarlanna video tawi thlir ho a ni a. Dr. Saurav Suman (Bihar), Asst. Professor, NIT Mizoram, Dr. Nidhi Yadav (Rajasthan), Asst. Professor, NIT Mizoram, Prof. Pravakar Rath (Odisha), Pro Vice Chancellor, MZU leh College of Veterinary Sciences & Animal Husbandry, Mizorama zirlai Sonali (Himachal Pradesh) te'n an state chanchin tawi an tarlang a. Mizoram University, NIT Mizoram leh Govt. Mizoram Law College zirlaite'n hnam lam leh zai mawi tak takte an entir bawk a. Hemi hnu hian Larsap hian hemi puala kalkhawmte hi Upper Lawn, Lok Bhavan-ah kawmin hun hlimawm tak an hmang ho a ni.

March ni 22, 1912 khan Bengal Presidency atangin Bihar a lo piang a. Rajasthan hi March ni 30, 1949 khan Jodhpur, Jaipur, Bikaner leh Jaisalmer lal ramte inzawmkhawm chuan siamin Greater Rajasthan ti a vuah a ni a. Odisha hi tunhma chuan Bihar hnuaiah awmin, April ni 1, 1936 khan state puitlinga hlankai a ni a, tawng hman behchhana India rama state piang hmasa ber a ni. April ni 15, 1948 khan Himachal Pradesh hi Indian Union hnuaia Part C State ni turin a lo piang a. January ni 25, 1971 ah state puitlinga hlankai a ni.

Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat programme hian India rama State leh UT hrang hranga cheng hnam hrang hrang, hnam ziarang leh nunphung inang lo tak takte inkarah inpawhna leh inunauna tha zawk hnam ziarang inhriatpawh tawnna kaltlangin siam a tum a ni.

title: ALL MIZORAM VILLAGE COUNCIL ASSOCIATION (AMVCA) LOGO TLANGZARH A NI
content:
127/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl the 10th April, 2025 :All Mizoram Village Council Association (AMVCA) Logo tlangzarhna chu Dr. Lalngura Tlau, Director, Local Administration Department chuan a pisa ah vawi chawhnu dar 1:00 khan a nei a. He hun hi Pu Lalchawimawia, Joint Director LAD chuan a kaihruai a, All Mizoram Village Council Association (AMVCA) hruaitute leh Local Administration Department (LAD) hotuten he hunah hian Inkawmna hun an nei nghal a ni.

Local Administration Department Director chuan Village Council-te’n khawtlang hmasawnna tur atana an lawmman lam ngaihtuah miah lo a theihtawp an chhuah thin leh sawrkar kut ke tangkai leh pawimawh ber an ni reng thei chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a. Mizoram sawrkar in VC-te hmasawnna tur a ngaihpawimawh zel avangin an Department pawhin hmachhawp lian tak tak an neih thu a sawi a. Ram hmasawnna tur atana a lungphum pawimawh hmasa ber VC-te chu theih tawp chhuah a hmasawnna kawng zawh zel turin a fuih bawk a ni.

Pu Lalhmingmawia, Jt. Director pawhin thu sawiin Department nen thawhhona tha nei a, ram hmasawnna tura theih tawp chhuah ho zel turin AMVCA hruaitute chu a sawm bawk. Pu FP Laikhama, President AMVCA chuan LAD a thawktute zawng zawng VC-te tana an in hawn avangin leh 15th FC pawisa an dawn theih nana an thawhrimnaah lawmthu a sawi bawk a ni.

He hunah hian Pu Lalropuia, General Secretary AMVCA chuan  kalkhawmte inhmelhriattirna leh AMVCA Logo Design sawifiahna a nei a. AMVCA chuan Logo hi a duh apiang tana Design thei turin ruahmanna siam niin, chung Design thehluh zingah chuan Pu V. Lalnunsanga, Graphic ART Design chu Director LAD ho in ni 2 April 2026 khan thlan a lo ni tawh a. Village Council hian tunhmain Logo an la nei ngai lo a, tuna Logo tlangzarh tak hi an Logo hmasa ber tur a ni.

title: MSPMC thuchhuah
content:
125/2025-2026

Aizawl, the 10th April, 2026 :Lunglei leh Buarpui inkar kawng Upgradation & Maintenance of Lunglei - Kawlhawk - Buarpui road, (0/00-66/500 km)' hi kawng pawimawh tak a nih avangin Mizoram State Project Monitoring Committee (MSPMC) pawhin a ngai pawimawh hle a. Specification tling ngeia thawh a nih theih nan a hmunah kalin a lo tlawh tawh nghe nghe a. Project hi a hlawhtlin ngei theih nan MSPMC chuan rawtna te pawh MPWD ah thlen tawhin, contractor te pawh kovin a lo sawi pui tawh bawk a. Hna hi chik takin a vil zui reng bawk a ni.

Tahrik ni 8, Tau thla, 2026, 'Zonet JB Chanchinthar, Lunglei a puan chhuah:- Specification tling lova contractor - Inckah Infrastructure Technologies Pvt Ltd., New Delhi ten an thawh hai khawk leh veka, Mizoram State Project Monitoring Committee (MSPMC) te beisei leh duh dan ang ngeia thawk turinMPWD, Lunglei Division ten hma an la tih MSPMC chuan a lo hria a. Hetianga Mizoram PWD te hmalakna hi tha a tin lawmawm a ti hle a. Hna hi quality tha leh kut tling ngeia thawh zawh a duh hle bawk a ni.

A tawp thlenga specification tling ngeia hna hi thawh zawh a nih theih nan MSPMC chuan kan vil zui zel dawn a ni.

Attachment

title: Champhai South MLA Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga leh Agricultural Technology Management Agency (ATMA) Champhai District tangkawpin fur thlai chi sem
content:
124/2025-2026

Champhai, 10th April 2026 :Champhai South bialtu MLA Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga chuan Agricultural Technology Management Agency (ATMA) Champhai District tanpuinain vawiin khan fur thlai chi a sem a, semna hun hi chawhnu dar 2:00 khan Circuit House, Champhaiah neih niin MLA chu khuallian a ni.

Khuallian Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga chuan thu sawiin a bial chhungah thlasik thlai hlawhtling taka kalpui a nih tawh hnu in ATMA te nen tangkawpin Fur thlai chi sem leh ni se tih a ni a. Thlai chi an sem chu a chi ṭha a nih thu sawiin loneituten theitawp chhuaha hlawhtlinna en tlak ngei a thawk tura chahin theihtawpa a vil zui tur thu a sawi. He hunah hian vaimim, zikhlum leh tomato chi tha Farmers 300 chuang hnena sem a ni.

He inkhawmah hian Dr. R. Zosangkima BLOCK Technology Manager ATMA Champhai chuan technical report pein ATMA chu loneitute tan an awm reng dawn thu a sawia, lawmthu sawina hun neiin inkhawm tih tawp a ni.

title: Mizoram Public Service Commission huaihawt Circle Officer under Social Welfare, Women and Child Development Department Examination, 2026 chungchanga Lawngtlai Bawrhsap thuchhuah
content:
123/2025-2026

Lawngtlai, 10th April 2026 :Mizoram Public Service Commission huaihawt Circle Officer under Social Welfare, Women and Child Development Department Examination, 2026 exam chungchangah Lawngtlai Bawrhsap chuan a hnuaia mi ang hian thuchhuah a siam.

PUBLIC NOTICE

Mizoram Public Service Commission huaihawt Circle Officer under Social Welfare, Women and Child Development Department Examination, 2026 on 14th April 2026 chu Govt. Lawngtlai College, Lawntlaiah neih tur a ni.

Candidate zawng zawngte Exam ni hian zing dar 8: 30 AM ah exam na hmun thleng theuh tawh tur a ni a, Admit Card ken theuh bawk tur a ni. Candidate zawng zawngte Frisking tih a ngaih avangin, thlen hun tur dar bi tukah thlen kim vek tur a ni.

Candidate zawng zawngte thawmhnaw mumal tak inbel tur a ni a, kekawrbul, Slipper etc inbel loh tur a ni. Tin exam centre- ah ran hruai loh tur a ni.

Exam Hall- ah hian Electronic Gadget eng chi mah ( Mobile Phones, Digital Watch, Smart Watch, Lighter etc ) ken luh phal a ni lo. Hei bakah hian thil bo hlauhthawnawm leh kan mamawh loh thil ken loh hram ni se. Keng lui kan awma a lo bo palh a nih chuan Exam Centre- a Officials leh duty ten mawh kan phur lo ang

Lirthei keng turte tan Gate pawnah lirthei dah vek tur a ni ang.

Sd/-

DONNY LALRUATSANGA

Deputy Commissioner

&

District Level Observer

Lawngtlai District, Lawngtlai

title: Pu Vanlalhlana PWD Minister-in East Tuipui Bial Khaw hrang hrang inkalpawhna kawng dinhmun a enfiah
content:
122/2025-2026

Dated Champhai, 10th April 2026:Vawiin khan Mizoram PWD Minister Pu Vanlalhlana chuan Vangchhia, Leithum, Dungtlang, Khawbung, leh Farkawn khaw chhung kawng thawmthat tur a ruahmanna kal mek chu hmunah kalin a enfiah a, PWD Department hotu pawimawh leh technical officer-ten an tawiawm a ni.

He hunah hian Minister hian Farkawn khawtlang hruaitute kawmin khawtlanga hmasawnna tak tak thleng tur chuan sorkar leh mipui tanrual a pawimawh ber tih a sawi a. Sorkarin sum tam tak sengin hmasawnna hna thawk thin mah se, a hmun ngeia mipuite'n neitu rilru pu chunga an thlawp leh an vil ve loh chuan hmasawnna chu a tlo thei lo tih a tarlang a. Khawtlanga hmasawnna rahbi thar kai tur chuan khawtlang hruaitute, tlawmngai pawl leh sorkar hnathawkte lungrual taka an tanrual a ngaih thu a sawi.

Pi Caroline Zoramthangi chuan DIET Mamit-a zirlaite leh thawktute fuihna thu a sawi a, thawktute chu thahnemngai taka zirlai naupangte hmakhua thlir chunga hnathawk tura chahin, zirlaite pawh taima taka an zirlai zir turin a chah bawk.

title: Mizorama tourist pung zel chungchangah Tourism Department hotute leh Central YMA aiawhte inkawm
content:
119/2025-2026

Aizawl, the 10th of April, 2026 :Vawiin chawhnu dar 1:00 khan Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar, Minister of State, Tourism Department hovin Tourism Department officer te leh Central Young Mizo Association (CYMA) hruaitute an inkawm a, Mizoram tlawhtu tourist pung zel lo dawnsawn leh inzirtirna kalpui kawngah thawhho theih dan tur an sawiho.

Tourism Minister Pu Lalnghinglova Hmar chuan Mizoram tlawhtu pung zel chu sum leh pai leh a tha zawnga Mizoram tarlan kawngah thil lawmawm tak a nih rual in, thil tha lo ken tel a neih theih avangin fimkhur taka lo dawnsawn a tul tih a sawi a. “Kum 2025 September ni 13 ah Sairang thleng rel a lo lut a, October thla atang khan chawhrualin thlakhatah mi nuaikhat chuang tourist kan la nei a ni. Kum 2025 chhungin tourist nuai 8 chuang an awm a, kuminah a aia tam an awm kan ring. Hengte hi kan culture leh khawtlang nunphung ti dal si lova mikhual kan lo dawnsawn a, inven tulna kawngah kan lo inven theihna’n nasa taka zirtirna kalpui a ngai a, YMA nen thawhho kan duh a ni.” a ti.

Minister chuan sawi chhunzawmin, “Tourism hi kan tihfuh chuan Mizoram tan industry lian ber pakhat a ni thei. Kan zavaia min nghawng avangin a dawnsawn kawngah pawh kan tanrual vek a ngai a ni.” tiin, Council of Churches in Mizoram leh PCI Mizoram Synod hotute nen pawh hemi chungchangah an lo inkawm tawh a, kohhran dangte leh NGO dangte nen pawh inrawnkhawmna hi neih chhoh zel tum a nih thu a sawi.

Pu R. Lalngheta, President, Central YMA pawhin Mizoram rawn tlawhtute’n hun hlimawm leh nuam tak hmang se an duh a, chutihrualin a sahawk lutuk leh a ram neitute awm tinuam lo zawnga khawsa an awm palh thei tih a tarlang. Chutiang a thlen loh nan, Mizoram mipuite bakah tourist te hriattur pawimawh, an cham chhunga an tih tur leh tih loh tur (Dos and don’ts) mumal tak siam tha a tih thu a sawi.

Vawiina inkawmhonaah hian inzirtirna (awareness campaign) kalpui chungchanga sawrkar leh Central YMA thawhho theihna hrang hrangte sawiho a ni a. Tourism kaihhnawiha eizawng hrang hrangte bakah, tourist te hriattur pawimawh buatsaih leh sem thin a nih thu leh, YMA in ngaih pawimawh zawng a neih chuan sawrkar a inhawn thu tarlan a ni.

Mizorama tourist site hrang hrangte hlutna leh nihna phochhuak tur leh, tourist te pawhin hlawk leh tangkai zawka hun an hman theihna’n Tour Guide training kalpui mek a nih thu tarlan a ni. Homestay hman dan dik tawk lo awm thin chungchang te, Inner Line Permit leh Protected Area Permit kalpui danah tourism thlirna atanga rawtna awmte sawiho a ni bawk. YMA branch hrang hrang atangin tourist te’n harsatna an tawh a an contact theih tur inruat theih a ni em tihte pawh sawiho a ni bawk.

Ram leh hnam tana hmasawnna turah chuan sawrkar tichak a, thawhpui turin Central YMA a inhawng a. Ziaka thawhhona tur Memorandum of Understanding ziah chungchang pawh an lo ngaihtuah tur thu CYMA aiawhte chuan an sawi a ni.

Meeting-ah hian Tourism Department atangin Pu Vanlaldina Fanai, Commissioner & Secretary; Pi R. Lalrodingi, Director leh officer dangte an tel a. Central YMA atangin President bakah Pu SF David Ngursangzuala, Asst. Secretary leh Pu MS Ralte, Financial Secretary te an tel bawk a ni.

Attachment

title: Lunglei District Buarpui khuaah Project LEAP hnuaiah vohbikte hriselna endik a ni
content:
118/2025-2026

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭau thla ni 10 :Lunglei District Bawrhsap hma laknaa Lunglei District-a vohbikte chawi kanna tur ruahmanna — Project LEAP (Lunglei's Empowerment for Access and Equity for All Persons) hnuaiah vawiin khan Bunghmun RD Block chhunga Buarpui Information & Cultural Centre-ah vohbikte hriselna dinhmun endikna (health assessment camp) buatsaih a ni.

He hun hi Bunghmun Block Development Officer Pu Paul Rokima chuan kaihruaiin, vohbikte hmangaiha ngaihsaktu District Bawrhsap an nei chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a. Project LEAP hi Lunglei District Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann hma lakna a nih thu leh nikum December ni 3 khan Lunglei District hian Project LEAP vangin National Award 2025 a lo dawng tawh tih a sawi.

Health Assessment Camp-ah hian Civil Hospital Lunglei aṭangin doctor speciallist leh mi thiam bikte'n vohbikte hriselna dinhmun an endik a. Social Welfare Department official-te chuan Unique Disability Identity (UDID) Card siamsakna turin vohbikte chanchin kimchang an la nghal bawk a. Mi 34 te chu UDID an neih theihna tur lehkha siamsak an ni.

Vawiin hian khaw paruk – Buarpui, South Khawlek, Bungtlang West, Dengsur, Kawlhawk leh Sertlangpui aṭanga camp pantu vohbik mi 37 te hriselna dinhmun endiksak an ni.

Attachment

PWD, Aizawl Road North Division, Sub Division-I chuan kawng siam that (junction improvement) hna an thawk dawn a, hnathawh chhungin a hnuai a tarlan kawng hi khar/block a ni dawn tih mipuite kan inhriattir e.

title: SIAHA DISTRICT NAOTLAH KHUAAH “MERA GAON MERA GAURAV” HUN HMANG
content:
116/2025-2026

Dated Siaha, 10th April 2026 :Krishi Vigyan Kendra, Siaha District buatsaihin vawiin khan Noaotlah - III (KM 10) khuaah Mera Gaon Mera Gaurav model village hun hman niin Lei hrisel tura enkawl dan leh leiṭha pek dan inzirtirna hun hman a ni.

Vawiina hun hman hi Pu Isaac Lallawmkima, Subject Matter Specialist (Agronomy) Krishi Vigyan Kendra, Siaha District in zirtirna pein a hmanpui a. Pu Isaac Lallawmkima leh Pu C. Vanlalawmpuia, Farm manager Krishi Vigyan Kendra, Siaha District-te chuan Soil testing tih dawna lei lak dante, dik taka leiṭha pek a pawimawh ziate, leilung da tawh leh chhe zel tur ven dante Farmers-te hnenah an zirtir bakah a taka entir theih chi chu a taka tihchhin nghal a ni.

He programme hi sawrkar laipuiin a ngai pawimawh êm êm a, Siaha district chhung khaw hrang hrangah zirtirna hi neih chhunzawm zel tura ruahman a ni. Hun hmannaah hian Noaotlah - III (KM 10) khuaa Lo/Huan neitute an tel ṭha hle.

Attachment

title: Lungleiah State Level Science Exhibition atana inpuahchahna ṭhut khawm buatsaih a ni
content:
115/2025-2026

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭau thla ni 10 :Mizoram State Council of Educational Research & Training (SCERT) Science Promotion Wing (SPW)-in kum tin an buatsaih ṭhin Rajya Stariya Bal Vaigyanik Pradarshani (State Level Student Science, Mathematics and Environment Exhibition tih ṭhin) chu May 7–8, 2026 khian Government High School, Lungleiah buatsaih leh tum a ni a. Hemi atana inpuahchah nan hian vawiin khan Lunglei Municipal Council (LMC) huam chhunga High School (HS) Headmaster leh Science subject nei Higher Secondary School (HSS) Principal te nen Lunglei Education Complex-ah thil pawimawh hrang hrang sawi ho a ni.

SCERT Deputy Director (SPW) Pu F. Lalliansanga chuan hun kaihruaiin, Science Exhibition kalpui a lo nih tawh dan te a sawi fiah a. Science exhibition-in naupangte thiamna tak tak a pek dan te sawiin, he programme hi National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 nena inhne rem thei ang bera kalpui a nih thu a sawi.

Lunglei District Education Officer & District Project Coordinator Pu Micky R. Lalenkawla chuan Lungleiah zirlaite hmasawnna atana hun ṭha leh remchang Science Exhibition buatsaih a ni tur chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin, zirtirtute chu a ṭha thei ang bera ṭawiawm leh thawhpui tur te, science exhibition-ah hian an zirlaite telpui ṭheuh tur te'n a chah.

He hunah hian State Level Science Exhibition buatsaihna tur leh a hlawhtlinna tura ṭul leh pawimawh hrang hrangte sawi ho a ni a. Exhibition thupui atan hian "STEM for Viksit and Atmanirbhar Bharat" tih chu hman a ni dawn a. State Level-a ti ṭhate hi National Science Exhibition-ah Mizoram ai awhin an tel leh ang.

Exhibition neih chhung hian "Reducing Plastic Pollution" (Plastic Bawlhhlawh Tihtlem) tih thupui hmangin Sciene tihlar dan tur zir hona, "One Day Seminar for Popularisation of Science" buatsaih a ni bawk ang.

Attachment

title: MLA Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga Champhaiah Mizoram Ex-Service League Assembly Vawi 19-naah khuallian
content:
114/2025-2026

Dated Champhai, 10th April 2026 :Champhai South bialtu MLA leh Mizoram Ex-Service League (MESL) Headquarters, Aizawl President ni bawk Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga chu vawiin khan Thangtea Hall, Champhai Kahrawt Venga Mizoram Ex-Service League (MESL) Sub-headquarters: Champhai Assembly vawi 19-naah khuallian a ni. He hun hi Honorary Captain C. Rohmingliana, MESL Sub-Headquarters Champhai chuan a kaihruai a, Champhai Bawrhsap Pu Mohammad Aaquib chu khual zahawm a ni.

Khuallian Lt. Col. Clement Lalhmingthanga chuan thu sawiin ex-servicemen hi Mizoram tana mi hlu tak tak an ni tih a sawi a, an chhungte zawng zawng nen ngaihtuah chuan Mizorama vote thlak theiah zaa sawm (10%) an tling a ni tih a sawi. India danpuia a inziah angin sipai nihna reng reng (rank) chu hminga bet reng tura phal sak an ni a, hei hian an hnathawh ropui zia leh hlut zia a tarlang tih a sawi a, Sainik Welfare and Resettlement department pawh ex-servicemen te hamthatna ngawr ngawr ngaihtuah tura ding a ni tih a sawi. Agniveer sipaia luh theihna tura pre-recruitment training thalai te tana buatsaih an tum thu te a sawi a, Territorial Army awm theih nan Tlangsamah hmalakna a kal mek zel a, hmanlai pi pute'n Zawlbuk an neih chhan pawh khua leh tui venghim turin a ni a, chutiang chuan Territorial Army awm tur pawh Champhai mipuite tana thlamuanna tur a ni tih sawiin mel lung pawimawh tak a ni ngei ang tih a sawi. Ex-services mi te hi an hlu a, MESL hian an hlutna an ti lang chhuak zel a ni tih sawiin he inkhawm hun hi hlim taka hmang turin a chah a ni.

He inkhawm hi hun hniha then a ni a, hun khatnaah hian MESL Champhai Secretary Pu Zonunsanga'n report a pe a, member sunna hun chairman-in a kaihruai. Khual zahawm Pu Mohammad Aaquib chuan thu a sawi a, MESL Champhai Vice-President Pu Lalparmawia'n lawmthu sawiin hun a khar. Hun hnihnaah hian infuihtharna leh thusawi hrang hrang ngaihthlak niin intihhlimna hun hman a ni a, MESL Sub-headquarters Champhai hruaitu thar thlanna hun hman a ni bawk.

MESL Sub-headquarters Champhai hi Mizoram Ex-Service Association anga din hmasak a ni a, MESL hnuaia Sub-headquarters nihna hi June 14, 1996 khan an chang a. Tun dinhmunah hian Champhaiah unit 8 an awm mek a ni.

Attachment

title: From Remote Roots to Rising Wings: CATC-56 Ignites Dreams Among Youth of Central and South Mizoram
content:
17/2026-2027

Lunglei, 10 April 2026 :The Combined Annual Training Camp (CATC-56), being conducted by 2 Mizo Bn NCC, Lunglei at 2nd MAP, Luangmual from 06 to 15 April 2026, is progressing with remarkable enthusiasm and a deeper mission of shaping young lives. More than a routine training programme, the camp serves as a transformative platform for youth from remote and economically disadvantaged backgrounds, offering exposure, building confidence, and instilling a strong sense of national pride.

With a total strength of 241 cadets—including an encouraging participation of nearly 50% girls—CATC-56 stands as a true reflection of inclusivity and grassroots empowerment. Many cadets have travelled from far-flung rural areas, overcoming significant challenges to be part of this life-changing experience, demonstrating that determination knows no boundaries.

The camp gained momentum with a motivating inaugural address by the Camp Commandant, Colonel Sagar K. Mohanty, on 07 April 2026. This was followed by an insightful guest lecture on "Modes of Entry into the Indian Army" delivered by Lt Col. Sahibjit Singh, Officiating Commanding Officer, 23 Assam Rifles. These sessions have sparked new aspirations among cadets, many of whom are now inspired to dream beyond their circumstances.

Importantly, this phase of training is also being utilised as an intensive preparation period for upcoming events such as the NCC Grand Fest and the Parasailing Camp. Cadets are being groomed physically, mentally and socially to represent their region with confidence on larger platforms.

The camp continues with a well-structured schedule comprising drill, map reading, weapon training, games and cultural activities. It also features a series of informative guest lectures on topics such as Media Awareness & Cyber Security, POCSO Awareness, Firefighting & Disaster Management, and Excise & Narcotics Awareness.

A notable highlight is the session on Media Awareness & Cyber Security delivered by Pu C. Lalhruaitluanga, a distinguished NCC alumnus and currently Deputy Director of Information & Public Relations (I&PR), Lunglei District. His journey from cadet to a key government position serves as a powerful source of inspiration for the participants.

CATC-56 is steadily emerging as a beacon of hope for rural youth—transforming limitations into opportunities and aspirations into determination. It sends a strong message that with the right guidance and platforms like NCC, even those from the most remote corners can rise, lead and serve the nation with pride.

With each passing day, the camp is doing more than training cadets—it is nurturing confidence, character and a brighter future for the youth of the region.

title: Lungleiah 2 Mizo Bn NCC huaihawtin ni 10 awh tur NCC Combined Annual Training Camp (CATC-56) buatsaih a ni
content:
113/2025-2026Lunglei, 2026 Ṭau thla ni 10 :2 Mizo Battalion NCC (National Cadet Corps) chuan April ni 6, 2026 aṭang khan 2nd Mizoram Armed Police Complex, Luangmual, Lungleiah NCC cadet-te tan April ni 15, 2026 thleng (ni 10) awh tur Combined Annual Training Camp (CATC-56) an buatsaih.

CATC-56-ah hian Junior Division (JD), Junior Wing (JW), Senior Division (SD) leh Senior Wing (SW) cadet 241 telin, a zatve (50%) dawn lai chu hmeichhia an ni a. Camp Commandant, Colonel Sagar K. Mohanty chuan cadet-te fuihna thu sawiin camp hi a hawng a. 23 Assam Rifles Officiating Commanding Officer, Lt Col. Sahibjit Singh chuan cadet-te hmaah sipai (Armed Force)-a luh dan kawng hrang hrang a sawi a. NCC cadet lo ni tawh ṭhin, Lunglei DIPRO Pu C. Lalhruaitluanga chuan media pawimawhna leh media hman dan ṭha bakah, internet kal tlanga pawi khawihna (cyber crime) laka himna chungchang a zirtir bawk.

CATC-56 hi May thlaa Lungleia buatsaih tur, NCC Grand Fest & Parasailing Camp atana inpuahchana atan hman nghal a ni a. Camp buatsaih chhung, ni sawm chhung hian NCC cadet-te chuan sipai lam (drill), silai leh ralthuam chelek, ram lem (map) en dan, ramhnuaia chettlat dan, infiam leh taksa sawi zawi, hruaitu ṭha ni tura thil pawimawh leh khua leh tuite mawhphurhna hrang hrang an zir dawn a. Mi thiam sawm bikte'n thupui hrang hrang – POCSO Act, kangmei laka himna leh chhiatna thlen thulha chet lak dan, vaihlo leh ruihhlo ṭhat lohzia leh thupui pawimawh tak tak hmangin cadet-te fuihna leh zirtirna hun an hman ṭhin bakah, hnam ziarang pho chhuahna hun te buatsaih a ni bawk ang.

title: THENZAWL PEACE CITY ATANA DILPUI CHHEHVEL RAM RUAHMAN DAN TUR NGAIHTUAHIN SERCHHIP DISTRICT LEVEL GOVERNMENT LAND AUTHORITY THUKHAWM
content:
112/2025-2026

Dated Serchhip, the 10th April, 2026 :Vawiin khan Serchhip District Government Land Authority chu an Chairman, District Bawrhsap Pu Paul L. Khuma hovin DCs Meeting Hall-ah an thukhawm a. Meeting-ah hian Thenzawl Peace City (Greenfield Project) hmalakna kal zela sorkar ram hman dan tur leh District chhunga hmasawnna hna hrang hrang thawh dante sawi ho a ni.

Meeting-ah hian Thenzawl Peace City hmalakna hnuaia, Dilpui area-a Animal Husbandry & Veterinary (AH&Vety) Department ram, 140 acre-a zau, ran chaw chinna hmun (fodder plantation) leh hnathawktute hmunhma (residential quarters) te chu, Urban Development & Poverty Alleviation (UD&PA) Department-in hmasawnna hna an thawhna tur atana hman tura ruahman a nih dan chungchangte sawi ho a ni. He hmun hi Neihloh khua-a sawn (relocate) tura rel tawh a nih laiin, AH&Vety Department hotute chuan Neihloh hi khawl hmanga huan leh rante enkawlna (mechanized farming) kalpui na atan chuan hmun remchang nia an hriat loh thu sawiin, thuneitu sang zawkte nen ngaihtuah that leh tura rel a ni.

Serchhip-a Women & Child Development Office sakna tur atana P&E Veng, Soil Department ram (UPC Biak In thlang) chu chiang zawka ennawn (location specific identification) leh ni se tih a ni bawk a. Chutih rualin, Leisang kawia Anganwadi Centre awm mek chu nunau leh naupangte tan a him tawk loh avangin, AOC Veng-a Soil Department ramah sawn tura ruahman a ni bawk.

Bawrhsap Pu Paul L. Khuma chuan sorkar ramte chu ram leh mipui tana vek a nih avangin uluk leh chak zawka humhalhna kalpui nih tur thu a sawi a. Ram ri hung (fencing) leh chhinchhiahna (demarcation) felfai tak neih a tul thu te, sorkar leh vantlang ram nek (encroachment) thinte lakah chak zawka hma lak a nih tur thute sawiin hun hi a khar a ni.

title: KAWRTHAH NORTH VILLAGE COUNCIL, MIZORAM WINS (1st RANK)THE NATIONAL AWARD UNDER THE THEME "NATIONAL AWARD FOR CLEAN & GREEN PANCHAYAT, 2025"
content:
16/2026-2027

Aizawl, 10th April, 2026 :Kawrthah North Village Council (LGD Code: 264555), Mizoram has been conferred the prestigious Deen Dayal Upadhyay Panchayat Satat Vikas, 2025 under the theme "Clean & Green Panchayat," securing the top rank at the national level.

The award recognizes the outstanding efforts of the Village Council in promoting sustainable development practices, environmental conservation, sanitation, and overall rural well-being. This achievement highlights the commitment of village council towards building an eco-friendly and sustainable village model.

The Ministry of Panchayati Raj, Govt. of India, has officially announced the recognition and appreciated the exemplary work carried out by the Village Council. The initiative reflects strong grass root governance and effective implementation of development, reinforcing the importance of community-driven initiatives in achieving long-term environmental and developmental goals.

The Rural Development and Administration Department, Government of Mizoram immensely appreciated the outstanding efforts and dedication undertaken by Kawrthah North Village Council and its community, while encouraging all other Village Council throughout the State to achieve utmost best in the coming years.

title: KAWRTHAH NORTH VILLAGE COUNCIL, MIZORAM CHUAN "NATIONAL AWARD FOR CLEAN & GREEN PANCHAYAT, 2025" PAKHATNA A DAWNG.
content:
111/2025-2026

Aizawl, 10th April, 2026:Kawrthah North Village Council (LGD Code: 264555), Mizoram chu India ram chhunga award zahawm ber pawl "Deen Dayal Upadhyay Panchayat Satat Vikas, 2025" hnuai ah "Clean & Green Panchayat," lawman pakhatna dawng tur a thlan a ni.

He lawmman a hmuhna hi "Clean & Green Panchayat" thupui bik atan a ni a, tehfung hrang hrang atang a endikna-ah mark hmu sang ber a ni.

Ministry of Panchayati Raj, Govt. of India chuan, Ni 30.03.2026 khan he award hlu tak hi a puangfel a, Kawrthah North Village Council chu khawtlang enkawlna kawng pawimawh hrang hrang changkanna rotling, rawmngaw humhalh, faina kawng leh thingtlang nun hmasawnna atana a tan laknate a hriatpui tak meuh a ni.

Prof. Lalnilawma, Hon'ble Minister, Rural Development and Administration Department, etc. Government of Mizoram chuan he award dawngtu Kawrthah North Village Council-te hi rilru leh thinglungtakin a lawmpui takzet a. Village Council member-te leh khawtlang mipui tanrualna leh mimal tin te taimakna hi a chhuang takzet a ni.

Village Council hrang hrang tepawh tan la lehzual a, mahni khawtlang theuh tan Kawrthah North Village Council hnathawh dan leh hmalakna te entawnin, hmasawnna neih belh zel a, award te pawh kum lo kaltur a dawng thei tura tan la zel turin a fuih bawk e.

title: Prof. Lalnilawma’n Zawlbuk Group YMA Inkhawmpui vawi 45-na hmanpui
content:
110/2025-2026Dated Aizawl: the 10th April 2026 :Vawiin khan Prof. Lalnilawma chuan Zawlbuk Group YMA inkhawmpui vawi 45-na, Seling Branch YMA Run-a neih chu a hmanpui.

He hunah hian Prof. Lalnilawma chuan thu sawiin, YMA chu Sawrkar Department nen tuihna humhalh kawnga thawk dun reng an nih avang leh pawl hlun ber pawl a nih angin, sawrkar pawhin a ngaihlu hle tih a sawi a. YMA-in kumpuan Ruihhlo do a kalpui pawh ṭha a tih thu sawiin, ram hmasawnna tura YMA hmalakna chu zahawm a tih thu a tarlang bawk a ni.

Prof. Lalnilawma chuan Zawlbuk group YMA huam chhunga khaw hrang hrangte tan, tui leh kawlpetha lama an hnianghnar leh zualna tura hmalakna kal mekte tarlangin, Vawmhraw tui lakna tur Project - Thingsulthliah khua, Tlangnuam leh Seling chawm tur leh Zawlbuk huam chhung khua ten kawlphetha hnianghnar zawka an hmuh theihna tur Thingsulthliah Power & Electricity Sub-Station tih changtlunna tur project te chu sawrkarin a pawm tawh thu a sawi bawk a ni.

Tun dinhmunah Zawlbuk Group YMA huam chhungah hian Branch 24 awmin, Member 11690 an  awm a. Central YMA hnuaia Group lian ber an ni mek a ni.

title: MAMIT MIPUITE HRIATTIRNA
content:
109/2025-2026Dated:Mamit the 10th of April, 2026 :Kan ram chhiarpui (Census), 2027- neih mekah a thawh khatna Phase-I atan intin chhiar (House listing) neih hmasak a ni dawn a. Mahnia lo inziahluhna (Self -Enumeration) online portal (se.census.gov.in) kaltlangin kan State chhungah kalpui mek a ni a. Mamit district mipuite pawh hun tiam chhung April ni 15, 2026 ral hma hian a tam thei ang ber inziak lut theuh tura ngen leh hriattir an ni.

title: Gas leh tuialhthei chungchanga Mamit District Bawrhsap thupek chhuah
content:
108/2025-2026

Dated : Mamit the 10th of April, 2026 :Mamit District chhungah tun dinhmunah Gas leh tuialhtheiah harsatna a awm rih lova, kan dinhmun te uluk taka vil reng niin, mipuite chiai lo tur leh thuthang lengvel satliah te awih lo turin kan inngen a. Hei bakah hian Delivery Authentication Code (DAC) chungchanga harsatna awmte pawh thuneitu sang zawkte hnenah thlen zel a ni a.

Chuvangin, mipuite social media-a thudik lo sawite awih mai lo tur leh harsatna kan tawk a nih chuan thuneitute hnena hriattir vat turin kan inngen a ni.

title: DRUG FREE DISTRICT CAMPAIGN LAUNCHED IN LAWNGTLAI
content:
15/2026-2027

Lawngtlai, 10th April 2026 :Today, the inaugural programme of the important “Drug Free District Campaign” under the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan was held at the DC Conference Hall. The programme was chaired by Shri Donny Lalruatsanga, Deputy Commissioner and Chairman of the Lawngtlai District Level Committee on Drug Free District. The campaign was officially launched by Shri C. Lalsawmzuala, Chief Executive Member (CEM), LADC.

Shri C. Lalsawmzuala expressed gratitude to the members of the District Level Committee and stated that the campaign was launched with the aim of making Lawngtlai District free from drug abuse and ensuring its successful implementation. He highlighted that since Lawngtlai is located along an international border, it is highly vulnerable to drug trafficking, making it essential to intensify efforts in combating drug abuse.He further stated that achieving a Drug-Free District would not be easy and would require strong cooperation and collective efforts from the government, Village Councils (VCs), NGOs, churches, and the general public. He urged everyone to work together with full commitment.

The CEM emphasized the need for careful and innovative approaches in tackling drug abuse. While acknowledging the continuous efforts made by the government, NGOs, and churches in rehabilitating drug users, he stressed that sustained dedication and perseverance are crucial. He reiterated that LADC will continue to contribute actively and called upon all stakeholders, including VCs, NGOs, churches, MJA, and the public, to take an active role in the fight against drugs.

Shri Donny Lalruatsanga highlighted that, alongside the initiatives of the Mizoram Government, this campaign is also a significant initiative of the Central Government. He mentioned that various important committees have been formed under this programme and that funds have been allocated by the Central Government to support anti-drug initiatives. He affirmed that all efforts will be made to transform Lawngtlai District into a Drug-Free District.

He also stressed that combating drug abuse requires collective action, noting that the efforts of Police, Excise, and NGOs alone are not sufficient. He called upon all citizens of Lawngtlai to actively participate in the fight against drugs.

Shri PC Lalchunglura, Superintendent of Police, in his speech appreciated the leadership of the Deputy Commissioner and emphasized that every individual has a significant responsibility in achieving a Drug-Free District or Village. He highlighted the importance of continued cooperation and collective effort in combating drug abuse.

Smt. Malsawmtluangi, Superintendent of DC Office, delivered a PowerPoint presentation explaining that the Drug Free Campaign, under the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan, aims to eliminate drug abuse and trafficking in Lawngtlai District. The campaign will cover 187 villages—99 under LADC and 88 under CADC. It is planned for a duration of three years and may be extended if necessary.Committees will be formed at the District, Block, and Village levels to carry out the campaign effectively within their respective jurisdictions. Villages declared as Drug-Free, with the approval of the District Administration, will be awarded Certificates of Recognition and Citations on every Independence Day.

The programme witnessed full participation from members of the Lawngtlai District Level Committee on Drug Free District. The event concluded with a vote of thanks delivered by Shri  Jammy Vanhmingliana, Sub Deputy Commissioner.
